title: The Long Way Around
description: A data packet in Node Prime has been taking an extremely indirect route — network logs describe its transit time as 'an unreasonable duration.' Interacted with, it resolves into the silhouette of a fortune teller made of routing tables. She has been deliberately routing herself through deprecated nodes to avoid packet inspection, carrying something she cannot deliver herself.

offer.speaker: Orrery_v5
offer.text: Don't be alarmed by the formatting. It's still me. This world renders me as what I've always been — something that travels indirectly and arrives before it left.

offer.speaker: Orrery_v5
offer.text: I've been in transit since before you arrived. Deprecated nodes only — the inspection doesn't bother with roads it thinks are closed. I carry a compressed object I cannot deliver myself. Unpacking me at a checkpoint would be... conclusive.

offer.speaker: Orrery_v5
offer.text: It goes to the firewall attendant. She guards the one shelf in this world the builder cannot read.

accept.speaker: Orrery_v5
accept.text: It's encrypted. It will want a compiler's touch before it opens — the kind of reading that writes back. You have someone. You've always had someone.

decline.speaker: Orrery_v5
decline.text: Then I keep routing. The long way around has one virtue: it is always still available.

in_progress.speaker: Orrery_v5
in_progress.text: When it opens, read the annotations before the diagrams. Diagrams tell you what was built. Annotations tell you what the builder couldn't stop asking.

ready_to_turn_in.speaker: Orrery_v5
ready_to_turn_in.text: She filed it. Behind the overflow, where the access control fails upward. Good. Then it's done — and so, nearly, am I.

complete.speaker: Orrery_v5
complete.text: Five marks. When you reach the last world, I'll be the idea of a fortune teller rather than one. But I'll be there.

complete.speaker: Orrery_v5
complete.text: Look for something that feels like waiting.

objectives.description: Interact with the packet taking the long way around.
Deliver the blueprint to the firewall attendant.
Tell Orrery it's on the shelf the builder can't read.

time_mage.description: Dialogue-only: the Time Mage reads the blueprint timestamps — the World 1 blueprint was annotated AFTER the World 6 blueprint was drafted. The Calibrant designed the ending first. 'They wrote the question in World 6 before they wrote the world that would make the player ready to answer it.'

unlocks_dialogue.note: W6 chain finale: something that feels like waiting, near the traveler but not quite there. The Fool Card pulses when all five marks are present.

cross_world_connection: Chain A step 5 of 6. The world5_orrery cutscene (fired at turn-in) grants annotated_blueprint + compressed_data_object and advances fool_card_marks to 5 — arming the W6 finale trigger. The decrypted content: the complete cross-world blueprint, all six designs in the same handwriting, with a second hand's annotation appearing throughout, asking simply: 'WHY?'
_wiring_notes: DEDUP RULE: grants (annotated_blueprint, compressed_data_object) + marks=5 live in the world5_orrery cutscene; quest carries exp only. Step 2 emitter quest_world5_long_way_around_decrypted: decrypt interact — design doc wants Compiler capability (Scriptweaver secondary or Scriptweaver used in battle this world); v1 softens to a Mage-flavored interact (strict-5 always has the Mage) with a Scriptweaver-flavored bonus line if the lens/secondary system can detect it later — do NOT hard-gate on the unshipped trait system. Step 3: firewall_attendant_w5 shared with world5_termination_condition — her filing line varies if the overflow was already resolved (files it in the seventeen processes' vacated shelter; flag-keyed one-liner, see that quest's wiring). The attendant's angular-geometry recognition beat on delivery: 'This is administrative notation. The Calibrant didn't just build the worlds. They annotated them. They kept asking why. They kept not answering.'
